reviews.review_description: The background audio is so loud I cant hear the narration. Its also very clumsy to move around room and menu is difficult to do basic navigation.  It was frustrating not being able to start, stop or move to another chapter or section of location. If there is an instruction section or info regarding these topics somewhere I would very much appreciate access to that.

The museum area is brilliant, although moving around isn't smooth. However the disconcerting VR photos, aren't good. The photos are to small. The 3D element is very strange. And with the photos, I'm not sure if I'm looking at real photos or a model village as the photos aren't pin sharp. As more work goes into it, I'm sure it will get better.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! Yes, I have a very small family team and of course we are doing everything possible to realize our dream in the best quality. I would be grateful if you can find our discord channel VR-Travel and write your suggestions for improvement so that I can add them to the update. 
Yes, you are right, we are also very much waiting for the development of VR to reach clearer screens, now this is the maximum possible resolution for Quest 2 so that nothing slows down. But on the Rift version, when connected via a computer, it turns out to achieve better quality, please email me: info@vr-travel.space and I will send you access to the Rift version there the image quality is noticeably better!

reviews.review_description:
First impressions
As soon as I started the game it started downloading. Understandable, but it would be nice if the downloads are in the game already instead of downloading afterwards. For the rest the booting went perfectly smooth and no performance issues whatsoever.

Positives
The menus are straight-forward and easy to use. As soon as I started the experience and it loaded I was stunned, it's something I had never seen before. The 3d pictures are super cool, the details are absolutely amazing. The "tour guide" had a nice clear voice and was easy to follow. As a non-native english speaker the information is good to follow, no complaints there whatsoever.

Negatives
The first thing I noticed was the movement in the main menu. It's a little disorienting and not very easy to use. I would personally prefer the standard joystick smooth walk movement or the vive teleporting. The smooth rotation is nice though. It would also be nice to have the pictures a little closer to you because I really felt like leaning in constantly, maybe a setting for it or something. For the rest, no complaints, overall super cool!

Looking forward to the future.

developer_response.body:
Very very cool and detailed review thank you so much!!!
Yes, now movement is like the flight of a ghost, I'm still thinking how to make it smoother. And in the new update, I want to implement the ability to zoom in the images!
If you have the opportunity, you can find channel VR-Travel in the discord, I will publish news about the update there.
Yes looking forward to the future. The main thing is that I have enough strength to do everything I have in mind!

reviews.review_description: It's my first experience in VR museums and i'm not disappointed!)

developer_response.body: Thank you, we are glad to please!

reviews.review_title: I am sure this not immersive at all
reviews.review_description: This is a app that can spin your head because you might think when is immersive tour going to begin. While all throughout we are only looking at small scale 3 D frames. How dumb. It wasted my hours of time trying to figure out when will immersive 360 experience begin. Low and behold it's only 3d photo frames to look at. Unless I have missed something which I don't think I have. This app is meant for computers but a tease for VR.

The museum is okaish thats all.
Firstly would be great if the images/packages were preloaded so that you don’t have to just sit around and wait for the download.
Secondly the experience is not immersive at all, just looking at 3D images, would also have been better if you could move around.Feels weird looking at tiny models through a small window 
The narration is good

reviews.review_title: I don't feel like I went there.
reviews.review_description: To start off this app is a great concept and can improve alot before the full release. All the narration was great and I learnt alot. The big problem with this app is you feel like a giant an everyrhing looks so small and it feels really weird having a black circle arlund tour vision and not being able to look around when you turn your head. I think this should be an option as some users experience motion sickness fairly easily but it just didn't feel immersive. Another thing which could be done but would take a lot more work is the addition of being able to move around the venue using the controlls as right now I had the same experience as a 2d you tube video. The graphics seem to be nice and it looks like a great start, but sadly it dosen't seem to be for me right now but I hope that can be changed. P.s. if you make any modifications feel free to contact me so I can check it out.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed feedback! Now I am preparing an update and thanks to your feedback I will add the ability to "turn on the light" in the black room!
And you're right, it was because of the fight against seasickness that we came to this kind of application, now it definitely doesn't get seasick! And I hope in the summer I will finish the option with moving through the pictures, then the experience will become more exciting!
If you have the opportunity, you can find our channel VR-Travel in the discord, I will publish news about the update there.

reviews.review_description: App contains only one museum room and poor quality 3d photos (not even 180/360). Waste of time.
